skill.name: youtube-downloader
skill.description: Download single YouTube videos or audio files with selectable quality, output format, and destination folder options.
skill.summary: Download YouTube videos or MP3 audio through a local yt-dlp helper script.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: The static subprocess findings in scripts/download_video.py are true positives for elevated operational risk, but no malicious intent was found. The SKILL.md shell findings are documentation examples and the weak cryptography alerts at SKILL.md:3 and scripts/download_video.py:105 are false positives. Runtime pip installation, network downloads through yt-dlp, and caller-controlled output paths warrant a medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Runtime Package Installation Modifies Environment
medium_findings.description: The helper script automatically installs yt-dlp with pip and uses --break-system-packages when the command is missing. This creates supply chain and environment integrity risk during normal skill use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call directly invokes pip install at runtime with a package name and --break-system-packages. This is legitimate setup behavior, but it modifies the Python environment and depends on package index trust.

medium_findings.title: External Downloader Runs Against User-Supplied URLs
medium_findings.description: The script passes the provided URL to yt-dlp for metadata retrieval and download. Argument lists avoid shell injection, but the skill performs network access and processes remote media metadata from user-provided locations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is caller-controlled and reaches yt-dlp through subprocess.run. The absence of shell=True reduces command injection risk, but network retrieval and third-party parser exposure are confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Caller-Controlled Output Directory
medium_findings.description: The script accepts an output directory and interpolates it into the yt-dlp output template. This can write downloaded files outside the default output directory if the caller supplies a different path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The output path is an argparse option and is used directly in the output template. The behavior is documented, but there is no path restriction to the default outputs directory.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Are Static False Positives
low_findings.description: The shell-command detections in SKILL.md are fenced usage examples for invoking the helper script. They are not executable code inside the skill file itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown fenced bash examples and option descriptions. They document commands that a user may choose to run, but SKILL.md does not execute them.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain descriptive text, not hashing or encryption code. No evidence of cryptographic operations was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md:3 is frontmatter description text, and scripts/download_video.py:105 is an argparse description string. Neither location imports crypto libraries or calls a cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded YouTube URLs Are Benign Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs in SKILL.md are sample YouTube links used in documentation. No evidence found that they exfiltrate data or contact unrelated endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only inside complete examples for a YouTube downloader. They point to YouTube watch URLs and are not connected to credential handling or hidden network behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run Used For External Tooling
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses subprocess.run to execute yt-dlp and pip. The calls use argument arrays rather than shell strings, so shell injection risk is reduced but external command execution remains a risk factor.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact subprocess.run calls are present in the helper script. Their list-form arguments make the risk operational rather than a direct shell injection finding.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automatic Dependency Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The script installs yt-dlp automatically if it is not present. Runtime installation can change the environment and relies on the package source available at execution time.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The installation path is explicit in check_yt_dlp. The package name is fixed, but the runtime installation behavior is confirmed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unrestricted Output Path Option
dangerous_patterns.description: The output directory option is passed into the download output template without restriction to a safe workspace path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The output argument is documented and wired directly into the output template. There is no visible validation or path allowlist in the file.

content.value_statement: Saving a YouTube video or audio track often requires choosing quality, format, and destination manually. This skill wraps yt-dlp with clear options for single-video downloads and MP3 extraction.
actual_capabilities: Downloads a single YouTube video using a local Python helper script.
Selects quality from best, worst, 1080p, 720p, 480p, or 360p.
Saves video output as MP4, WebM, or MKV.
Uses a custom output directory when requested.
Fetches video title, duration, and uploader before downloading.
limitations: Only single videos are supported because playlists are disabled by default.
The script depends on yt-dlp and may install it at runtime.
Downloads can fail when YouTube changes access rules or blocks a video.
Users must ensure they have rights to download and store the media.

use_cases.title: Save reference videos for review
use_cases.description: Download a single video in a predictable format for later offline review.

use_cases.title: Extract audio for transcription
use_cases.description: Create an MP3 from a video so a separate transcription workflow can process it.

use_cases.title: Prepare media files for editing
use_cases.description: Choose a resolution and container format before importing a video into editing tools.

prompt_templates.title: Download one video
prompt_templates.prompt: Download this YouTube video in the best available MP4 quality and save it to the default output folder: [URL]

prompt_templates.title: Choose video quality
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a smaller video file
prompt_templates.prompt: Download this YouTube video as 720p MP4 and tell me the saved filename when it finishes: [URL]

prompt_templates.title: Extract MP3 audio
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs audio for a follow-up workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Download only the audio from this YouTube video as MP3 and save it in the default output folder: [URL]

prompt_templates.title: Specify format and folder
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user needs a specific output format and location
prompt_templates.prompt: Download this YouTube video as 1080p WebM and save it to this folder: [OUTPUT_FOLDER]. URL: [URL]

output_examples.input: Download a YouTube video in 720p as MP4.
output_examples.output: The skill reports the video title, duration, uploader, selected quality, format, and output folder before saving the file.

output_examples.input: Extract audio only from a YouTube video.
output_examples.output: The skill saves an MP3 file and confirms that the audio-only download completed.

output_examples.input: Download a WebM file to a custom folder.
output_examples.output: The skill uses the requested output directory and format, then reports whether the download succeeded or failed.

best_practices: Use trusted YouTube URLs and verify that the download is permitted.
Keep output paths inside a workspace or user data directory.
Choose lower resolutions when file size or download time matters.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to download copyrighted media without permission.
Do not point output paths at system or application directories.
Do not assume playlist downloads will work, because playlists are disabled by default.

faq.question: Does this skill download playlists?
faq.answer: No. The script passes a no-playlist option, so it is intended for single videos.

faq.question: Can it download audio only?
faq.answer: Yes. The audio-only option extracts audio and saves it as an MP3 file.

faq.question: Which video formats are supported?
faq.answer: The script exposes MP4, WebM, and MKV as output format choices.

faq.question: Where are files saved by default?
faq.answer: Files are saved to /mnt/user-data/outputs unless the user supplies another output directory.

faq.question: Does it need internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses yt-dlp to fetch video metadata and download media from YouTube.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It has medium operational risk because it runs external commands, downloads network content, and may install yt-dlp at runtime.
